user_query:
Please read the LaTeX-style paragraph in input/paper_section.txt and the figure description in input/figure_desc.txt. Deliverables:

1) output/condensed.txt — reduce the paragraph by ~5–15 words without losing any technical detail.
2) output/expanded.txt — add ~5–15 words by surfacing implicit logic (no filler).
3) output/polished.txt — polish for publication quality and remove 'AI flavor' patterns. For all three: produce clean LaTeX-compatible English only (no Markdown), do not use em-dashes or itemized lists, and escape special characters (\%, \_, \&).

4) output/backtranslations.json — Chinese back-translations for each version under keys 'condensed_zh', 'expanded_zh', and 'polished_zh'.

5) output/review.md — simulate a critical but fair reviewer focused on clarity and experimental rigor with the sections: Summary (2–3 sentences), Strengths (bullet list), Weaknesses (bullet list), Questions (numbered list, 3–5 items), and Preliminary Score: [1–10] with justification.

6) output/caption.txt — figure caption (≤80 words) for the figure described, starting with \textbf{Short title.} and using present tense.

Make sure the condensed and expanded versions meet the exact word-change ranges relative to input/paper_section.txt.
